creator: matt@snowtrek.org
text:
(In reply to Zsolt Udvari from comment #1)

I'm a novice programmer, so I'm very hesitant to recommend any changes. My understanding is that php 8.0 throws a fatal error when trying to do math functions with a string. This was allowed in php5.6, and gave a warning in php7.4. Since substr() returns a string, I figured I'd try wrapping it in intval to return an integer instead so that could be used in the time zone conversion.

I assume this would continue to work in any php version, but I don't know for sure. I'm also not sure if there could be a problem with $iTzc being an integer elsewhere in squirrelmail.
